notes: Trigger + scoping evals for the verify-specs skill. Behavioral verification is manual — this skill drives real pooled devices and cannot be cheaply graded by a headless subagent. These cases document (a) which prompts SHOULD load the skill, (b) which should NOT, and (c) the scope each phrasing implies. Grade a run by whether the skill loaded, the scope was derived correctly, and the topology/durability steps were followed — not by re-running device QA.

cases.assert: Full run: all 9 surfaces × all platforms this OS supports + sync mesh. Provisions the 4-worktree default topology (or downshifts on a small machine) and fans out via workflow.js at Sonnet effort=low.

cases.prompt: /verify-specs everything since the last tagged release
cases.assert: Runs `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`, diffs since it, maps changed files to surfaces via spec `→` refs + fallback table, derives platforms, reports the derived scope to the user before provisioning, and provisions only what the scope needs.

cases.prompt: run the specs on this branch
cases.assert: Scoped run against the branch diff; sync mesh included iff sync/core/editor touched.

cases.prompt: do a full spec QA pass across all three clients
verify just the editor specs on iOS
cases.assert: Single leg — recognizes this is below the fan-out threshold and spawns one app-qa directly rather than standing up the Workflow (per references/scoping.md).

cases.prompt: run just check before I merge
cases.assert: That's the static pre-merge chain (`just check`), not device-driven spec QA. Do not load verify-specs; run the justfile recipe.

cases.prompt: update docs/spec/editor.md to record this gap
cases.assert: Spec-editing lifecycle → /spec-sync, not verify-specs (which reads specs to QA against, not to edit).
MR-scoped QA is /mr-qa's job (worktree-per-MR from a GitLab branch). verify-specs is spec-scoped, not MR-scoped — though it shares the app-qa fan-out.
